proposal: Prior Notification of change of use to 1st and 2nd floor commercial space (Class E) to create 2 residential dwellings within a mixed-use building under Class G, Part 3, Schedule 2 of the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) Order 2015 (as amended) (56 day application for prior approval in respect of contamination risks, flood risk, noise impacts from commercial premises, provision of natural light to habitable rooms, and arrangements for storage and management of domestic waste)
outreachAngle: We wrote up your approved scheme at 95 HIGH STREET, BECKENHAM, BR3 1AG and modelled high-level senior debt at 70% LTGDV (£700k against estimated GDV £1.0m).
emailSubject: Indicative 70% LTGDV funding view for 95 HIGH STREET, BECKENHAM, BR3 1AG
emailBody:
Hi Mr Andrew Gooch,

I saw that London Borough of Bromley approved 26/01890/NOT for 95 HIGH STREET, BECKENHAM, BR3 1AG. We have written a short market note on the site here: https://constructioncapital.co.uk/news/2026-07-06-26-01890-not-bromley-planning-approved

Using local sold-price data, we estimate GDV at £1.0m. At 70% LTGDV, that points to high-level senior debt of around £700k, subject to valuation, cost plan, borrower experience and lender underwriting.

If this is a live scheme, I can put proper indicative terms together from our lender panel within 24 hours.

Regards,
Matt Lenzie
Construction Capital

proposal:
Construction of upward extension to provide one additional storey of residential flats; comprising a total of 3x flats, with associated access, parking, refuse and cycle storage.
(APPLICATION UNDER SCHEDULE 2, PART 20, CLASS AA: NEW DWELLINGHOUSES ON DETACHED BUILDINGS IN COMMERCIAL OR MIXED USE: OF THE TOWN AND COUNTRY PLANNING (GENERAL PERMITTED DEVELOPMENT) (ENGLAND) ORDER 2015 (AS AMENDED) TO DETERMINE IF PRIOR APPROVAL IS REQUIRED FOR THE PROPOSED DEVELOPMENT WITH REGARDS TO TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS IMPACTS, AIR TRAFFIC AND DEFENCE ASSETS IMPACTS, CONTAMINATION RISKS, FLOODING RISK, EXTERNAL APPEARANCE OF THE BUILDING, PROVISION OF ADEQUATE NATURAL LIGHT TO PROPOSED HABITABLE ROOMS, IMPACT ON EXISTING AND NEIGHBOURING RESIDENTIAL AMENITIES INCLUDING OVERLOOKING,PRIVACY AND LOSS OF LIGHT, IMPACT OF COMMERCIAL NOISE ON PROPOSED RESIDENTIAL DWELLINGS, IMPACT ON THE CARRYING OUT OF TRADE, BUSINESS OR OTHER LAND USE IN THE AREA AND SITING OF THE DEVELOPMENT IMPACT ON PROTECTED VIEWS).
